an1.100:1.1: "Taking internal factors into account, *bhikkhus*, I see no other single factor that leads to such great harm as this: laziness.
an1.101:1.1: "Taking internal factors into account, *bhikkhus*, I see no other single factor that leads to such great benefit as this: the arousal of energy.
an1.101:1.2: The arousal of energy, *bhikkhus*, leads to great benefit."
an1.102-109:1.1: "Taking internal factors into account, *bhikkhus*, I see no other single factor that leads to such great harm as this: having many wishes … having few wishes … discontent … contentment … inappropriate attention [*ayoniso manasikāra*] … appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikāra*] … lack of clear comprehension [*sampajānā*] … clear comprehension [*sampajānā*] … the twelfth.
an1.110:1.1: "Taking external factors into account, *bhikkhus*, I see no other single factor that leads to such great harm as this: bad friendship.
an1.110:1.2: Bad friendship, *bhikkhus*, leads to great harm."
an1.111:1.1: "Taking external factors into account, *bhikkhus*, I see no other single factor that leads to such great benefit as this: good friendship.
an1.111:1.2: Good friendship, *bhikkhus*, leads to great benefit."
an1.112:1.1: "Taking internal factors into account, *bhikkhus*, I see no other single factor that leads to such great harm as this: devotion to unwholesome *dhammas* and non-devotion to wholesome *dhammas*.
an1.112:1.2: Devotion, *bhikkhus*, to unwholesome *dhammas* and non-devotion to wholesome *dhammas* leads to great harm."
an1.113:1.1: "Taking internal factors into account, *bhikkhus*, I see no other single factor that leads to such great benefit as this: devotion to wholesome *dhammas* and non-devotion to unwholesome *dhammas*.
an1.113:1.2: Devotion, *bhikkhus*, to wholesome *dhammas* and non-devotion to unwholesome *dhammas* leads to great benefit."
an1.114:1.1: "I see no other single *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, that leads to the confusion and disappearance of the true *Dhamma* as this: heedlessness.
an1.114:1.2: Heedlessness, *bhikkhus*, leads to the confusion and disappearance of the true *Dhamma*."
an1.115:1.1: "I see no other single *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, that leads to the stability, non-confusion, and non-disappearance of the true *Dhamma* as this: heedfulness.
an1.115:1.2: Heedfulness, *bhikkhus*, leads to the stability, non-confusion, and non-disappearance of the true *Dhamma*."
an1.116:1.1: "I see no other single *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, that leads to the confusion and disappearance of the true *Dhamma* as this: laziness.
an1.116:1.2: Laziness, *bhikkhus*, leads to the confusion and disappearance of the true *Dhamma*."
an1.117:1.1: "I see no other single *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, that leads to the stability, non-confusion, and non-disappearance of the true *Dhamma* as this: the arousal of energy.
an1.117:1.2: The arousal of energy, *bhikkhus*, leads to the stability, non-confusion, and non-disappearance of the true *Dhamma*."
an1.118-128:1.1: "I see no other single *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, that leads to the confusion and disappearance of the true *Dhamma* as this: having many wishes … having few wishes … discontent … contentment … inappropriate attention [*ayoniso manasikāra*] … appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikāra*] … lack of clear comprehension [*sampajānā*] … clear comprehension [*sampajānā*] … bad friendship … good friendship … devotion to unwholesome *dhammas* and non-devotion to wholesome *dhammas*.
an1.118-128:1.2: Devotion, *bhikkhus*, to unwholesome *dhammas* and non-devotion to wholesome *dhammas* leads to the confusion and disappearance of the true *Dhamma*."
an1.129:1.1: "I see no other single *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, that leads to the stability, non-confusion, and non-disappearance of the true *Dhamma* as this: devotion to wholesome *dhammas* and non-devotion to unwholesome *dhammas*.
an1.129:1.2: Devotion, *bhikkhus*, to wholesome *dhammas* and non-devotion to unwholesome *dhammas* leads to the stability, non-confusion, and non-disappearance of the true *Dhamma*."
an1.129:1.4: The group of four is concluded.
an1.130:1.1: "Those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, who present what is not-*Dhamma* as *Dhamma* — those *bhikkhus* are practicing for the harm of the many, for the unhappiness of the many, for the loss, harm, and suffering of the many, of gods and humans.
an1.130:1.2: Those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, accumulate much demerit, and they cause this true *Dhamma* to disappear."
an1.131:1.1: "Those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, who present what is *Dhamma* as not-*Dhamma* — those *bhikkhus* are practicing for the harm of the many, for the unhappiness of the many, for the loss, harm, and suffering of the many, of gods and humans.
an1.131:1.2: Those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, accumulate much demerit, and they cause this true *Dhamma* to disappear."
an1.132-139:1.1: "Those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, who present what is not-*Vinaya* as *Vinaya* … who present what is *Vinaya* as not-*Vinaya* … who present what was not spoken or uttered by the Tathāgata as spoken and uttered by the Tathāgata … who present what was spoken and uttered by the Tathāgata as not spoken or uttered by the Tathāgata … who present what was not practiced by the Tathāgata as practiced by the Tathāgata … who present what was practiced by the Tathāgata as not practiced by the Tathāgata … who present what was not prescribed by the Tathāgata as prescribed by the Tathāgata … who present what was prescribed by the Tathāgata as not prescribed by the Tathāgata;
an1.132-139:1.2: those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, are practicing for the harm of the many, for the unhappiness of the many, for the loss, harm, and suffering of the many, of gods and humans.
an1.132-139:1.3: Those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, accumulate much demerit, and they cause this true *Dhamma* to disappear."
an1.132-139:1.5: The second chapter on heedlessness and so forth is the tenth.
an1.98:0.2: 10. The Second Chapter on Heedlessness and So Forth
an1.98:1.1: "Taking internal factors into account, *bhikkhus*, I see no other single factor that leads to such great harm as this: heedlessness.
an1.99:1.1: "Taking internal factors into account, *bhikkhus*, I see no other single factor that leads to such great benefit as this: heedfulness.
